front.title: Post-Quantum Cryptographic Agility Profile for ACME

abstract.t: This document defines a Post-Quantum Cryptographic (PQC) Agility Profile extension for the Automated Certificate Management Environment (ACME) [RFC8555]. The extension introduces a typed pqcAgility object that an ACME server includes in its directory metadata and that an ACME client may embed in an order object to express per-order PQC policy. The object carries: a ranked list of PQC algorithm preferences; a hybrid_required enforcement bit; a rotation_epoch_id binding certificates to a coordinated migration epoch; an optional composite_chain_policy identifier governing composite-signature chain construction; and an optional pqc_posture_score reflecting the requestor's cryptographic-readiness assessment.

This document defines the semantic content of a named PQC profile and is intended to complement the generic ACME Profiles Extension [I-D.ietf-acme-profiles], which specifies the profile-selection mechanism (an opaque profile name string in the order object) but does not define the PQC-specific fields that give that selection cryptographic meaning. It does not modify the core ACME state machine or account management flows defined in RFC 8555.
Multi-tenant ACME deployments MUST enforce tenant isolation of pqcAgility directives. Concrete isolation parameters are deployment-specific and are intentionally out of scope.

note.title: Relationship to Other Documents
note.t: This document is distinct from and does not conflict with draft-giron-acme-pqcnegotiation (expired 2024-08-19, individual submission), which defines a pre-account /cert-algorithms discovery endpoint and KEMTLS certificate issuance modes. That document addresses algorithm capability advertisement before account creation. This document addresses per-order PQC enforcement policy, hybrid mandates, rotation epoch binding, composite chain governance, posture scoring, and tenant isolation.

This document is also distinct from the ACME Profiles Extension [I-D.ietf-acme-profiles], which defines a generic, algorithm-agnostic profile selection string mechanism. This document defines the typed PQC semantic fields that populate a PQC-named profile within that framework. The two documents are complementary: an ACME server MAY expose a profile named (for example) "pqc-hybrid-ml-dsa-65" whose content and enforcement semantics are defined by this document, while the selection mechanism is provided by [I-D.ietf-acme-profiles].

note.title: Source and Archival
note.t: Source for this draft is maintained at https://github.com/Sanc-Admin/acme-pqc-agility-profile. A citable archival version of this document is published at Zenodo upon each tagged release. Author ORCID iD: https://orcid.org/0009-0006-6395-5308. Author professional credentials: https://www.credly.com/users/brian-vicente.
Discussion of this document occurs on the IETF "acme" mailing list (acme@ietf.org). Issues and pull requests may be filed at the GitHub repository linked above.

note.title: IPR Considerations
note.t: The author has filed United States patent applications covering subject matter relevant to this document, including U.S. Provisional Patent Application No. 64/080,137 (filed 2026-06-01) and U.S. Patent Application No. 19/698,870 (filed 2026-06-05). By posting this Internet-Draft, the author submits to the IETF Trust the rights described in Section 5 of BCP 78 and BCP 79. Patent licensing terms are not yet known. Implementers and reviewers should consult the IETF Datatracker IPR disclosure page for this document for current disclosure status. The Sanctum SecOps Private Enterprise Number (PEN) root used for any OID allocations referenced in companion documents is 1.3.6.1.4.1.65953.

Patent-Pending Claim Scope Notice: The pending patent applications referenced above cover orchestration, observability, and tenant-isolation mechanisms in multi-tenant PKI systems as they intersect the claims of those applications. The per-order pqcAgility object structure, rotation_epoch_id field semantics, hybrid_required enforcement bit, and pqc_posture_score field are among the elements relevant to those applications. This Internet-Draft does not grant any patent license. Implementation parameters that extend beyond the disclosed interface (including but not limited to scoring weights, rotation triggers, tenant carve-out parameters, and adequacy thresholds) are intentionally out of scope of this document.

section.name: Introduction
section.t: ACME [RFC8555] is the dominant protocol for automated certificate issuance. As the Internet migrates toward post-quantum cryptography (PQC) and hybrid certificate chains, ACME deployments require a structured way to express per-order PQC enforcement policy beyond simple algorithm capability advertisement.

Existing work addresses adjacent but distinct problems. The expired individual submission draft-giron-acme-pqcnegotiation defines a pre-account /cert-algorithms capability discovery endpoint and KEMTLS certificate issuance modes, but does not define per-order policy objects, hybrid enforcement mandates, rotation epoch bindings, composite chain policy, posture scoring, or tenant isolation requirements. The ACME Profiles Extension [I-D.ietf-acme-profiles] defines a generic, algorithm-agnostic profile selection mechanism using an opaque profile name string, but does not specify the PQC-semantic content those profiles carry.

This document fills the gap by defining the typed pqcAgility object -- the PQC-semantic payload that an ACME server advertises and an ACME client attaches to an order to enforce a specific cryptographic agility posture.

section.name: Terminology
section.t: The key words "MUST", "MUST NOT", "REQUIRED", "SHALL", "SHALL NOT", "SHOULD", "SHOULD NOT", "RECOMMENDED", "NOT RECOMMENDED", "MAY", and "OPTIONAL" in this document are to be interpreted as described in BCP 14 [RFC2119] [RFC8174] when, and only when, they appear in all capitals, as shown here.

dl.dd: An ordered list of PQC algorithm identifiers, most preferred first. The server MUST select from the intersection of advertised and requested algorithms. If the intersection is empty, the server MUST reject the order with an unsupportedPQCAlgorithm problem document.

When true in directory metadata, the server REQUIRES all certificates under this profile to use a hybrid composite signature. A client setting this true in an order asserts a requirement for hybrid issuance; the server MUST honor this or reject the order. This enforcement bit is distinct from the algorithm selection mechanism in draft-giron-acme-pqcnegotiation, which does not define a server-side mandatory hybrid enforcement gate.

An opaque server-defined string identifying the migration epoch cohort. Epoch identifiers coordinate certificate renewals across account populations toward a common PQC algorithm baseline. Not defined in any prior ACME draft.

A server-defined identifier referencing composite-chain construction rules governing which classical algorithm is paired with which PQC algorithm at each chain tier. Not defined in any prior ACME draft.

An integer in [0, 100] reflecting the cryptographic-readiness posture of the account or deployment. A score of 100 indicates full PQC-native capability. The scoring methodology is deployment-specific. Not defined in any prior ACME draft.

section.name: Directory Advertisement
section.t: An ACME server compliant with this profile MUST include a pqcAgility object in its directory metadata when offering PQC agility profiles. Example directory metadata fragment:

section.name: Order Object Extensions
section.t: An ACME client MAY include a pqcAgility member in a new-order request. Example new-order payload fragment:

section.name: Tenant Isolation
section.t: Multi-tenant ACME servers MUST ensure that pqcAgility directives from one account do not influence issuance decisions for other accounts. In particular:

ul.li: A rotation_epoch_id submitted by one account MUST NOT alter the epoch assignment of any other account.
A composite_chain_policy identifier supplied by one tenant MUST NOT cause non-default chain construction policy for other tenants.
A pqc_posture_score provided by one account MUST NOT be used as eligibility input for any other account.

section.t: Concrete tenant-isolation implementation parameters are deployment-specific and are intentionally out of scope of this document.

section.name: Relationship to Prior and Concurrent Work
Distinction from draft-giron-acme-pqcnegotiation
section.t: draft-giron-acme-pqcnegotiation-03 (expired 2024-08-19, no WG adoption) defines a /cert-algorithms discovery endpoint and KEMTLS issuance modes. It does not define: per-order policy objects; a hybrid_required enforcement bit; rotation epoch binding; composite chain policy identifiers; PQC posture scoring; or multi-tenant isolation requirements. This document addresses those gaps and is not a competing mechanism.

section.name: Relationship to draft-ietf-acme-profiles
section.t: The ACME Profiles Extension [I-D.ietf-acme-profiles] defines a generic mechanism for servers to advertise named certificate profiles and for clients to select a profile via an opaque string. It does not define PQC-semantic content. This document defines that content. Servers MAY expose PQC agility profiles named per [I-D.ietf-acme-profiles] whose enforcement is defined here. The pqcAgility object MAY appear alongside the profile field from [I-D.ietf-acme-profiles] in an order object.

section.name: Relationship to LAMPS and PQUIP Work
section.t: The hybrid_required bit and composite_chain_policy identifiers are designed to interoperate with [I-D.ietf-lamps-pq-composite-sigs] and [I-D.ietf-pquip-hybrid-signature-spectrums]. This document defines how an ACME server enforces and advertises selection of those formats via the order lifecycle.

section.name: Security Considerations
section.t: This document builds upon the security model of RFC 8555. The extensions do not modify account management or identifier validation flows. PQC posture metadata (pqc_posture_score) is sensitive. Servers SHOULD avoid exposing aggregated tenant PQC posture without authorization. The rotation_epoch_id field MUST NOT be used to correlate accounts across tenants. Servers SHOULD treat client-supplied posture scores as advisory only. The hybrid_required enforcement bit creates a migration dependency; server operators SHOULD announce hybrid-required profiles with sufficient lead time via rotation_epoch_id.

section.name: IANA Considerations
section.t: IANA is requested to create a new registry group, "Automated Certificate Management Environment (ACME) PQC Agility Profile", containing:

ul.li: "ACME PQC Composite Chain Policy Identifiers" (Expert Review)
"ACME PQC Agility Error Types": initial entry unsupportedPQCAlgorithm (Specification Required)

section.t: IANA is also requested to add pqcAgility (object) to the "ACME Directory Metadata Fields" registry and to the "ACME Order Object Fields" registry (Configurable: true). Reference: this document.

front.title: Key words for use in RFCs to Indicate Requirement Levels
Ambiguity of Uppercase vs Lowercase in RFC 2119 Key Words
Automated Certificate Management Environment (ACME) Profiles Extension
Composite ML-DSA for use in Internet PKI
Transition to Post-Quantum Cryptography Standards (NIST IR 8547)
